Now — the browser

Pull a drawer on the left
to watch the chaos unfold…
0 windows  ·  0 tabs open
Then One drawer, one purpose
Every card had a place. Pull a drawer — it opened. You read, you noted, you closed it. The cabinet waited, patient and solid.
Now One click, many rabbit holes
Every link opens a new tab. Every tab says "see also." Every "see also" opens three more. The cabinet has gone feral.
Then "See Also" meant one card
A cross-reference was a single index card pointing elsewhere. You walked to that drawer. Deliberate. Slow. Controlled.
Now "See Also" means ten new tabs
Every webpage has twenty links. Click three and you've lost the original page. The information is still there — the cabinet just multiplied.
The reassuring truth: You mastered the card catalogue when it felt intimidating to everyone around you. The browser is the same challenge — same virtue, new form. Patience and system still win. You're not starting from zero.
